to rush together (hastily assemble) or headlong (figuratively)
to crush completely, i.e. to shatter (literally or figuratively)

a sign in common, i.e. preconcerted signal
a fellow-insurgent
to impale in company with (literally or figuratively)

to split or sever (literally or figuratively)

en: a split or gap ("schism"), literally or figuratively
to save, i.e. deliver or protect (literally or figuratively)
kjv: heal, preserve, save (self), do well, be (make) whole

en: the body (as a sound whole), used in a very wide application, literally or figuratively
to be of sound mind, i.e. sane, (figuratively) moderate
kjv: be in right mind, be sober (minded), soberly

en: the fresh, i.e. young girl; talitha (O maiden)
to stir or agitate (roil water)
shortly, i.e. without delay, soon, or (by surprise) suddenly, or (by implication, of ease) readily
a child (as produced)
an artificer (as producer of fabrics), i.e. (specially), a craftsman in wood
to finish life (by implication, of G979 (βίος)), i.e. expire (demise)

properly, the point aimed at as a limit, i.e. (by implication) the conclusion of an act or state (termination (literally, figuratively or indefinitely), result (immediate, ultimate or prophetic), purpose); specially, an impost or levy (as paid)
a tax-farmer, i.e. collector of public revenue
a tax-gatherer's place of business
a prodigy or omen
forty
four
fourth
four times a thousand
in a far-shining manner, i.e. plainly

perhaps akin to G2334 (θεωρέω)); to guard (from loss or injury, properly, by keeping the eye upon; and thus differing from G5442 (φυλάσσω), which is properly to prevent escaping; and from G2892 (κουστωδία), which implies a fortress or full military lines of apparatus), i.e. to note (a prophecy; figuratively, to fulfil a command); by implication, to detain (in custody; figuratively, to maintain); by extension, to withhold (for personal ends; figuratively, to keep unmarried); by extension, to withhold (for personal ends; figuratively, to keep unmarried)
kjv: hold fast, keep(- er), (pre-, re-)serve, watch

en: to place (in the widest application, literally and figuratively; properly, in a passive or horizontal posture, and thus different from G2476 (ἵστημι), which properly denotes an upright and active position, while G2749 (κεῖμαι) is properly reflexive and utterly prostrate)
kjv: + advise, appoint, bow, commit, conceive, give, X kneel down, lay (aside, down, up), make, ordain, purpose, put, set (forth), settle, sink down

Timæus (i.e. Timay), an Israelite
to prize, i.e. fix a valuation upon; by implication, to revere
some or any person or object
kjv: a (kind of), any (man, thing, thing at all), certain (thing), divers, he (every) man, one (X thing), ought, + partly, some (man, -body, - thing, -what), (+ that no-)thing, what(-soever), X wherewith, whom(-soever), whose(-soever)

en: an interrogative pronoun, who, which or what (in direct or indirect questions)
kjv: every man, how (much), + no(-ne, thing), what (manner, thing), where (-by, -fore, -of, -unto, - with, -withal), whether, which, who(-m, -se), why

en: truly this, i.e. of this sort (to denote character or individuality)

probably itself from the base of G5056 (τέλος) through the idea of extreme conduct); to venture (objectively or in act; while G2292 (θαῤῥέω) is rather subjective or in feeling); by implication, to be courageous

a spot (general in space, but limited by occupancy; whereas G5561 (χώρα) is a large but participle locality), i.e. location (as a position, home, tract, etc.); figuratively, condition, opportunity; specially, a scabbard
kjv: coast, licence, place, X plain, quarter, + rock, room, where

en: the when, i.e. at the time that (of the past or future, also in consecution)
a table or stool (as being four-legged), usually for food (figuratively, a meal); also a counter for money (figuratively, a broker's office for loans at interest)
the throat (neck), i.e. (figuratively) life
"three"
to "tremble" or fear
compare G2359 (θρίξ)); which uses (the base of G1408 (δρόμος)) as alternate in certain tenses; to run or walk hastily (literally or figuratively)
thirty
three hundred
akin to , , and the base of G5131 (τράγος), G5134 (τραῦμα)); a rut or worn track
to creak (squeak), i.e. (by analogy) to grate the teeth (in frenzy)
three times
third; neuter (as noun) a third part, or (as adverb) a (or the) third time, thirdly
a "trembling", i.e. quaking with fear
a bowl
akin to the base of G5134 (τραῦμα), G5147 (τρίβος) and G5176 (τρώγω)); an orifice, i.e. needle's eye

to "thump", i.e. cudgel or pummel (properly, with a stick or bastinado), but in any case by repeated blows; thus differing from G3817 (παίω) and G3960 (πατάσσω), which denote a (usually single) blow with the hand or any instrument, or G4141 (πλήσσω) with the fist (or a hammer), or G4474 (ῥαπίζω) with the palm; as well as from G5177 (τυγχάνω), an accidental collision); by implication, to punish; figuratively, to offend (the conscience)
opaque (as if smoky), i.e. (by analogy) blind (physically or mentally)
healthy, i.e. well (in body); figuratively, true (in doctrine)
water (as if rainy) literally or figuratively
a "son" (sometimes of animals), used very widely of immediate, remote or figuratively, kinship
you (as subjective of verb)
to hymn, i.e. sing a religious ode; by implication, to celebrate (God) in song
to lead (oneself) under, i.e. withdraw or retire (as if sinking out of sight), literally or figuratively
to hear under (as a subordinate), i.e. to listen attentively; by implication, to heed or conform to a command or authority
to go opposite (meet) under (quietly), i.e. to encounter, fall in with
"over", i.e. (with the genitive case) of place, above, beyond, across, or causal, for the sake of, instead, regarding; with the accusative case superior to, more than

superabundantly, i.e. exceedingly
an under-oarsman, i.e. (generally) subordinate (assistant, sexton, constable)
under, i.e. (with the genitive case) of place (beneath), or with verbs (the agency or means, through); (with the accusative case) of place (whither (underneath) or where (below) or time (when (at))

something bound under the feet, i.e. a shoe or sandal
down under, i.e. beneath
